data.description: Graduate Student Association – Coffee & Breakfast Event The Graduate Student Association (GSA) invites all graduate students to join us for a morning of coffee, breakfast, and community. data.description: As a graduate student, teaching assistant, instructor, or friend, you may wonder about what constitutes a disability, academic accommodations, and accessibility. The Office of Disability Services is hosting a drop-in afternoon for graduate students interested in learning about the services that ODS provides. Stop by the Graduate Student Lounge or join via Zoom and meet Coordinator Nychey Michel to discuss classroom accommodations. Bring yourself or bring a friend to ask questions and learn about outreach efforts. Coffee and donuts will be provided while supplies last! Questions or accommodation requests specific to this event? Please email dsoffice@echo.rutgers.edu, call 848-202-3111, or visit ODS in Lucy Stone Hall, Suite A145, Livingston Campus.
data.name: Graduate Student Disability Services Drop-In Hours

data.description: The Rutgers Association of Marketing and Strategy (MAR) is teaming up with Milan for an exciting and insightful Mallu Talk! This collaborative event brings together culture, creativity, and career conversations in one engaging space. Join us as we dive into navigating professional growth, marketing, media, and identity as young Malayali students balancing tradition and ambition. Whether you’re interested in business, branding, entrepreneurship, or simply connecting with like-minded peers, this event is a great opportunity to learn, network, and celebrate our community. Come ready for meaningful discussion, shared experiences, and a fun, welcoming atmosphere! Food, networking, and good vibes included
data.name: Mar X Milan Mathew Mallu Talk

Whether you enjoy classic board games, strategic challenges, or simply want to hang out with fellow students in a relaxed setting, this is the perfect opportunity for you.What to Expect: - Casual and competitive board games - A welcoming and inclusive environment - A fun break from academic stress Feel free to come solo or bring a friend. data.description: We have an exciting collaboration coming up on March 5th! From 7:30-9:00pm, join us for a thrilling evening with many activities aimed to immerse you into the wonderful world of accents, false cognates, and more linguistic-y topics! Free food and beverage will be available. We are tentatively planning to be in the College Ave Student Center. We will communicate the final location information at a later date.We look forward to seeing you!Nous sommes ravis d’annoncer une collaboration avec @rulinguistics prévue pour le 5 mars ! De 19 h 30 à 21 h, prenez part à une soirée animée avec des jeux et des activités axés sur le monde de la linguistique, c’est-à-dire les accents, les faux amis et bien plus encore. Boissons et collations offertes gratuitement.Pour l’instant, nous prévoyons que l’événement aura lieu au College Avenue Student Center. Nous communiquerons le lieu exact ultérieurement.Nous sommes ravis de vous accueillir pour une autre belle soirée !

data.description: Join us for a visit to the United Nations to learn more about the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The visit will consist of a briefing by the UN Principles for Responsible Management Education (PRME) Office, followed by a tour of the UN. This trip is Part 1 of a two-part SDG Challenge at Rutgers, with an event on April 3rd as Part 2 (you do not have to attend Part 1 to attend Part 2).   Please RSVP by Monday, February 23rd. Once you RSVP and your registration is confirmed, we ask that you bring a $5 cash refundable deposit to the Rutgers Global Office (30 College Ave, New Brunswick, NJ, 08901) to confirm your spot before the deadline.    Lunch will be provided on the trip.   Co-Sponsors: Rutgers Business School (RBS) and Innovation, Design, and Entrepreneurship Academy (IDEA).
data.name: UN SDG Trip

data.description: Dear Graduate Students and Faculty in Life Science and Biomedical Programs,   Abstract submission is open for graduate students to present at the 2026 Annual Graduate Student Research Symposium, sponsored by the Molecular Biosciences Graduate Student Organization (MBGSO) and Rutgers School of Graduate Studies.    Date: Friday, March 6th, 2026 Time: 9:30 am - 4:30 pm  Location: Life Science Building Atrium, Busch Campus Keynote Speaker: Joseph Contessa, MD, PhD, Chair of Radiation Oncology, Head of Translational Research, Rutgers Cancer Center    Please use this link or the QR code in the flyer attached to register to attend and/or submit an abstract.  Note that abstract submission deadline is January 30th, 2026.    We are looking for faculty to judge the posters and talks. If you are able to help, please contact Prateeksha Rout as soon as possible. pr398@gsbs.rutgers.edu   Everyone attending the symposium including students, faculty and postdocs must register by March 2nd in order to receive a boxed lunch.    We look forward to seeing you there!   -Your Molecular Biosciences Graduate Student Organization (MBGSO) Board
data.name: Annual Graduate Student Research Symposium

data.description: Drop-in for an Artful Saturday monthly event hosted by the Center for the Women in the Arts at Douglass and Scarlet Arts RX! In this Women's History Month themed workshop, let your imagination take shape and make a mini rage piñata. Artsy door prizes! Bring a friend! Taught by a Scarlet Arts Rx student teaching artist from Rutgers Mason Gross School of the Arts. Free supplies, snacks, and boba provided! Supplies are limited – First come, first served. All skill levels and walk-ins welcome!  Foundational art skills you will learn at this event: How to construct a piñata, An introduction to the concept of the glass ceiling, Key ideas from Women’s History Month. This experience counts towards the Douglass Discovery curriculum requirement. R3: Career/Discovery/Professional Development/Research. This event is part of the Douglass' 2026 Week of the Woman Series. Week of the Woman is a dedicated series that honors women’s impact and advances women’s leadership through community-engaged programs that inspire, educate, and mobilize Douglass students. Co-sponsored by the Douglass Community Engagement office.Reminder: To receive DRC curriculum credit, you must complete a reflection survey. ___ * Image and Media Release By attending this event, you acknowledge and agree that Rutgers University may photograph, record, or otherwise capture your artwork, image, and/or voice. You grant Rutgers the unrestricted right to use, reproduce, publish, and distribute these sound, still, or moving images in any medium for educational, promotional, advertising, or other purposes aligned with the University’s mission. All rights to these materials will belong to Rutgers University. Please let our photographer or videographer know if you do not want your image taken at the event.
data.name: Douglass x Scarlet Arts RX | Artful Saturday: Smash the Glass Ceiling Mini Rage Piñatas

data.description: This event is open to all Douglass Students!   Join the Pharmacy and Nursing Living and Learning Community for a fun Sip, Snack, and Stabilize event. Discover how everyday snacks and drinks can influence your body’s balance of fluids and minerals. You’ll get to mix and match different snack/drink options to see how they help the body stay balanced and healthy. It’s a fun, hands‑on way to learn a bit of science while enjoying tasty treats and exploring what balance really means. Wellness made simple.   Stop by to refuel, recharge with a chance of winning some prizes if you can come up with a winning balancing combo!   Image and Media Release Disclosure:  By attending this event, you acknowledge and agree that Rutgers University may photograph, record, or otherwise capture your image, and/or voice at this event. You grant Rutgers the unrestricted right to use, reproduce, publish, and distribute these sound, still, or moving images in any medium for educational, promotional, advertising, or other purposes aligned with the University’s mission. All rights to these materials will belong to Rutgers University.   Please let our photographer or videographer know if you do not want your image taken at the event.
data.name: Hydration & Health: A Clinical Electrolyte Experience

data.description: Open to all Douglass Residential College students! Join us for an exciting evening at the Bunting-Cobb Lounge featuring Ingredion, a global ingredient solutions company that transforms plant-based materials into the ingredients used in many of the foods and drinks you enjoy every day. From snacks, sauces, soups, yogurt, and ice cream to cereals, baked goods, beverages, and plant-based products, Ingredion works behind the scenes to perfect taste, texture, sweetness, and sustainability. This is a great opportunity to see how STEM connects directly to real-world products — and to explore internships and career paths in engineering, R&D, manufacturing, supply chain, sustainability, and more. What to Expect:• Learn about Ingredion and their global impact• Explore internship and career opportunities• Play Ingredion Jeopardy for a chance to win DRC swag• Make your own DIY lava lamp• Enjoy free food and connect with friends Date: Monday, March 9th, 2026 Time: 7:30 PM  Place: Bunting-Cobb Lounge Photo/Video Release By attending this event, you (including minors under the age of 18) are also agreeing to give Rutgers University permission to record your image and/or voice and grant Rutgers all rights to use sound, still, or moving images in any medium for educational, promotional, advertising, or other purposes that support the mission of the university. You agree that all rights to the sound, still, or moving images belong to Rutgers.
data.name: Glow & Grow with Ingredion: Careers, Lava Lamps & Free Food with the Sciences LLC!
organizationNames: Douglass Women in Science and Engineering (WiSE)
Bunting-Cobb Sciences Living-Learning Community

data.description: All BME-affiliated undergraduates, graduate students, and postdocs are invited to present their research through either posters or oral presentations. The symposium is open to the entire BME department, including non-BME students conducting research in BME labs. A panel of judges will assess both posters and presentations, and winners will receive a prize! Faculty interested in participating as judges, please reach out to us at rutgersbess@gmail.com to let us know! Please use this Google form to indicate your interest in presenting: https://forms.gle/9mdozEzRJ2NRjXgP8.   To submit an abstract, we will ask for the following: 1. Title, Authors, and Affiliations.2. An abstract (cannot exceed 250 words) that can include the following sections:   - Introduction   - Materials and Methods   - Results (If Applicable)   - Conclusions   Abstracts are due on 03/01/26 at 11:59 pm. All participants are welcome to present their posters, and those chosen for an oral presentation will be notified by the week of 03/08/26.  We hope you will join us to showcase our community's outstanding research, celebrate your achievements, and foster new collaborations and discussions! We will send out further details about the event schedule and logistics at a later date.
data.name: 7th Annual BESS Symposium

data.description: CLICK HERE TO REGISTER!   The Rutgers University Honors College is thrilled to present the 1st Annual Heart & Sole 5K at Buccleuch Park!   The race kicks off at 10:30 AM sharp, so come early to grab your bib and free event t-shirt! Pickup opens at 9:00 AM at the Large Pavilion. Every registrant will also earn a finisher medal as they cross the line!   Stick around afterward for our 11:30 AM Award Ceremony, where top finishers will be recognized with exciting awards. Extra prizes will also be raffled off!   All registration fees and donations will support the American Heart Association in honor of American Heart Month. Thank you for helping us make a meaningful impact! Registration is capped at 200 participants, so don’t wait—spots will fill fast!   Additionally, Honors College students will be awarded 2 community service hours upon request!
data.name: Heart & Sole 5K

data.description: Join the Center for Women in the Arts at Douglass for a workshop led by acclaimed artist Liz Collins! This unique needlepoint workshop invites you to explore the meditative joy of stitching. Collins will guide participants through the myriad of stitches possible within needlepoint and share her distinctive methodology for creating improvisational designs, treating needlepoint as a slow drawing process in response to the grid. All skill levels and forms of stitching are welcome and supported.  Only open to Douglass and Art & Design students. Free supplies and snacks provided! Supplies/snacks are limited – First come, first served. Space is limited. Please arrive on-time. This experience counts towards the Douglass Discovery curriculum requirement. R3: Career/Discovery/Professional Development/Research. Reminder: To receive DRC curriculum credit, you must complete a reflection survey. *Liz Collins is the 2025-2026 Estelle Lebowitz & Ann Beneduce-Lebowitz Artist-in-Residence. Her solo exhibition, Shapeshifter, is on view through March 13, 2026 in the Mary H. Dana Women Artists Series Galleries at Douglass Libraries. ___ * Image and Media ReleaseBy attending this event, you acknowledge and agree that Rutgers University may photograph, record, or otherwise capture your artwork, image, and/or voice. You grant Rutgers the unrestricted right to use, reproduce, publish, and distribute these sound, still, or moving images in any medium for educational, promotional, advertising, or other purposes aligned with the University’s mission. All rights to these materials will belong to Rutgers University. Please let our photographer or videographer know if you do not want your image taken at the event.
data.name: Needlepoint Workshop with artist Liz Collins
data.organizationName: Academic Programs at Douglass Residential College - DRC

data.description: Tu museo. Tu idioma. / Último Domingo es una serie que celebra la cultura latina e invita a las comunidades bilingües a unirse a través del arte. Explora nuestras deslumbrantes exposiciones, crea arte y diviértete con funciones interactivas. Gratis y abierto al público. Contamos con el generoso apoyo brindado por el programa Access for All de Art Bridges Foundation. 22 de febrero 29 de marzo Your museum. Your language. / Último Domingo is a series celebrating Latinx culture and invites bilingual communities to come together through the experience of art. Explore our stunning exhibitions, create art, and enjoy interactive performances. Free and open to the public. The Art Bridges Foundation’s Access program provided generous support for bilingual text in the All program. February 22 March 29
data.name: Último Domingo | Last Sunday

data.description: As a graduate student, teaching assistant, instructor, or friend, you may wonder about academic accommodations and accessibility. The Office of Disability Services is hosting a drop-in afternoon for graduate students interested in learning about the services that ODS provides. Come stop by the Graduate Student Lounge or join via Zoom and meet Coordinator Nychey Michel to discuss accommodations for: hearing, vision, and mobility conditions, learning disabilities, psychological diagnoses (such as anxiety or depression), chronic illnesses, traumatic brain injuries, ADHD, Autism Spectrum Disorder, and more. Bring yourself or bring a friend to ask questions and learn about outreach efforts. Coffee and donuts will be provided while supplies last!   Questions or accommodation requests specific to this event? Please email dsoffice@echo.rutgers.edu, call 848-202-3111, or visit ODS in Lucy Stone Hall, Suite A145, Livingston Campus.
data.name: Graduate Student Disability Services Drop-In Hours
organizationNames: Graduate Student Life (Department at Rutgers University)
Office of Disability Services (University Department)

data.description: Every 1st and 3rd Thursday of each month! We start again on 2/5/26!  Join us for a creative space where students can explore self-expression through art. Each session offers guided prompts as well as the freedom to create in your own style and medium—whether drawing, painting, writing, or any other form of artistic expression. This group encourages open dialogue, reflection, and connection while using art as a tool for self-discovery and communication.
data.name: Let's Talk With Art

data.description: This co-organized event between Rutgers and Princeton University marks the inaugural meeting of scholars from both institutions focusing on Abya Yala, the Kuna term for the indigenous territory that encompasses what is now known as Latin America.  Taking up the notion of “trans-indigenous” methodologies as proposed by Chadwick Allen – that is, thinking about the indigenous beyond the imposition of colonial borders– our gathering explores connections across indigenous communities throughout the Americas, and invites scholars from across the region to participate in this conversation.  With an interdisciplinary focus, the event brings together topics such as indigenous literature, film, ancestral medicinal practices, and oral testimony to examine how indigenous knowledge systems and traditions circulate and resonate across borders, languages, and communities. We will be joined by guest keynote speaker, poet and professor Odi González (NYU), who will address the complex process of translating oral Quechua literature into written languages, such as Spanish and English. The event will finish with a film screening of Javier Corcuera's Sigo siendo (2013), a documentary which dives into the heterogenous musical expressions of Peru, influenced by local cultures and repertoires from both indigenous and Afro-Peruvian roots. With a focus on migrant subjects in the industrial capital, the film highlights the impossibility of conceiving contemporary indigenous societies in isolation, celebrating the diverse community ties they create as they work to maintain their cultural vibrancy.
data.name: Trans-Indigenous Abya Yala  1st Dialogue in Indigenous Studies at Rutgers-Princeton University
data.organizationName: Literary Studies in Spanish Organization (Graduate Organization)

data.description: Spring is here.  Join the Rutgers Global Student Volunteer Corps for a fun evening of flower making.  Our student leaders will teach attendees how to create beautiful flowers out of paper, pipe cleaners, and other materials.  By the end of the evening, you will leave with a small bouquet of realistic looking flowers to enjoy on your own or share with someone else.
data.name: Spring Blooms: Craft Night

data.description: Please join us on April 18, 2026 for the Rutgers Tree Planting Festival, where we will be planting 3000 trees using the Miyawaki Method in place of an abandoned roadway that was removed last year by Rutgers Institutional Planning and Operations. The goal of this event is to build community by collectively contributing to the environment and giving students first-hand experience in tree planting. There will be trees, art, and music!  This event is open to all members of the Rutgers community!  Sign up for the 10:00am-12:00pm, 12:00pm-2:00pm, or 2:00pm-5:00pm. (But you can come and go as you please!) Please RSVP using this link: https://rutgers.ca1.qualtrics.com/jfe/form/SV_41L7gvHGcte3Q2y
data.name: RU Tree Planting Festival

data.description: Drop-in for an Artful Saturday monthly event hosted by the Center for the Women in the Arts at Douglass and Scarlet Arts RX! Join us for a Spring painting picnic in Jameson courtyard (rain location: Ludwig Center). Artsy door prizes! Bring a friend and a blanket to sit on (if you have one)! Taught by a Scarlet Arts Rx student teaching artist from Rutgers Mason Gross School of the Arts. Free supplies, snacks, and boba provided! Supplies are limited – First come, first served. All skill levels and walk-ins welcome! Foundational art skills you will learn at this event: Painting and referencing nature, Color theory and color relationships. This experience counts towards the Douglass Discovery curriculum requirement. R3: Career/Discovery/Professional Development/Research.Reminder: To receive DRC curriculum credit, you must complete a reflection survey. ___ * Image and Media Release By attending this event, you acknowledge and agree that Rutgers University may photograph, record, or otherwise capture your artwork, image, and/or voice. You grant Rutgers the unrestricted right to use, reproduce, publish, and distribute these sound, still, or moving images in any medium for educational, promotional, advertising, or other purposes aligned with the University’s mission. All rights to these materials will belong to Rutgers University. Please let our photographer or videographer know if you do not want your image taken at the event.
data.location: Jameson Courtyard / Schonberg Rm, Ludwig (rain location)
data.name: Douglass x Scarlet Arts RX | Artful Saturday: Painting Picnic

data.description: Come join us on Sunday, April 19, for Haru's biggest event of the semester, Spring 2026 Showcase! Our goal is to share Korean pop culture through the art of dance. This event is open to everyone: general members, families, Rutgers students, and non-Rutgers students! Our showcase is our biggest event, where our members get to showcase a multitude of K-pop performances they’ve been preparing for months for our audience. There are two acts with an intermission, which includes a raffle with a bunch of prizes our audience can partake in and possibly win big! And the best part is that admission is FREE for all!  You don't have to be performing to attend, so come out and support our performers! ♡ Let's cheer each other on and enjoy each other's company! You may also get some sweet treats, snacks, and refreshments from us to enjoy while watching performances 👀 Our showcase is also livestreamed on our YouTube page for audience members who cannot attend in person, but would love to support our performers! If you're watching from online, thank you for supporting Haru Dance ♡ More information on parking will be detailed here later in the semester. Contact us directly on IG: @harudance_official or Email: haru.kdcc@gmail.com if you have any questions! Can't wait to see y'all soon!
data.location: Cook/Douglass - Trayes Hall Douglass Student Center
data.name: HARU Spring 2026 Showcase
